text.title: PM + Tech-Lead Grooming Agenda Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the pm-tech-lead-grooming-agenda methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Agenda MUST have exactly three blocks: Priorities, Tech-Debt, Capacity — no extra blocks.
rule.rationale: Extra blocks turn grooming into a status meeting and erode focus.

rule.statement: Debt block MUST honor the team's published debt-quota (e.g. 20% of capacity).
rule.rationale: Skipping debt erodes velocity within 2 quarters.

rule.statement: Every row groomed MUST exit with one of: promote / hold / reject; no 'we will discuss next time'.
rule.rationale: Deferred decisions accumulate and meeting time grows linearly.

rule.statement: If capacity-block overflow occurs, agenda MUST name the displaced item explicitly.
rule.rationale: Implicit tradeoffs hide cost from stakeholders.

rule.statement: Promote decisions MUST link to user/customer evidence or measurable hypothesis.
rule.rationale: Evidence-free promotes drift into HiPPO territory.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
